content:
nod or a tray of buns. But the cellar door is open now, and I am standing in the frame.

I look at Levi, really seeing the way he stands there, caught between the street and our table. I see the hesitation in the set of his shoulders, a mirror of the man I used to be—always looking for the quickest way back into the shadows. I reach out, my hand steady, and tap the surface of the table twice. The
